frameworks.name: inverse rational control (IRC)
frameworks.one_line: Infer an agent's internal model and reward by assuming rational action selection.

frameworks.name: partially observable Markov decision process
frameworks.one_line: Latent task variables evolve as Markovian dynamics underlying behavior.

frameworks.name: rational agent / subjective expected utility
frameworks.one_line: Agent maximizes subjective reward given its understanding of task causal structure.

core_question: How can behavior and neural activity reveal an agent's internal model and its dynamic latent variables?
key_assumptions: Agent behaves rationally under its own (possibly wrong) task model
Subjective reward and beliefs together determine action selection
Relevant task variables follow Markovian dynamics
manipulations: Simulated agents performing a naturalistic foraging task
Agents act suboptimally relative to the true task
key_findings: IRC recovers the simulated agents' internal model and reward function from behavior
Method extracts interpretable latent thoughts driving action
Markovian computational dynamics within the neural manifold representing task variables are recovered
interpretations: Behavior plus neural activity can be jointly explained by a rational agent with a learnable internal model
Encoding, recoding, and decoding of neural data can be framed relative to this rational behavioral model
limitations_noted: Demonstration is on simulated agents rather than empirical neural recordings
